title: Geoeconomics: 'All dependencies, minerals, currencies and semiconductors, are now being weaponized'
subhed: According to geopolitics expert Marc-Antoine Eyl-Mazzega, the competition for control over minerals, logistics and the flow of raw materials is benefiting the US and China. Europe lags behind in this geoeconomic race, with a tangible impact on how major powers are reshaping their defense capabilities.

title: Behind the cordial facade, the Trump-Xi summit yields no significant progress

Migrant rights: Council of Europe member states push back against the European Court of Human Rights
subhed: Prompted by several governments, including those led by Giorgia Meloni of Italy and Mette Frederiksen  of Denmark, the 46 member states have issued a political declaration aimed at tightening the European Court of Human Rights' (ECHR) case law on migrant rights.

title: Lebanon says truce extension with Israel and US-backed security create opportunity for 'lasting stability'
subhed: The Lebanese negotiating delegation said from Washington that the move would 'provide critical breathing space for our citizens' and 'reinforce state institutions.'

title: Eurovision 2026: Israel's participation sparks controversy
subhed: In an unprecedented move, five countries – Spain, Ireland, Iceland, the Netherlands and Slovenia – are boycotting the event in protest over the presence of an Israeli contestant. Pro-Palestinian demonstrations are also planned in Vienna, where the final will be held on Saturday, May 16.

title: 'People are hiding out at home, some don't even go to work anymore': Residents of El Paso, Texas try to evade ICE agents
subhed: Long present in this border city with Mexico, where 80% of the population is Hispanic, the US immigration enforcement agency has recently stepped up its operations and arrests. It has even opened a 5,000-bed detention center in the neighboring military zone.

How China is cracking down on its Christians
subhed: Le Monde verified footage from Chinese social media and collected testimonies from victims of Beijing's crackdown to reveal how the regime has brought independent churches to heel since Xi Jinping came to power.

title: Trump, with ambitions for Mars, Canada and Greenland, tries to reclaim frontier myth of pioneer America
subhed: Since the very beginning, the US has based its growth on the conquest of new geographical or political frontiers. In this spirit, the current president speaks of other lands – on Earth and in space – while the rise of artificial intelligence opens up uncharted territories.

title: After the 2025 disaster, Macron hopes to make his last year as president count
subhed: Emmanuel Macron, who will leave office on May 13, 2027, seeks to be remembered as a president who stayed active until the end. On the world stage, he recently regained some of the credit he lost to domestic challenges.
